summary.intent: Install the gstack tool into a project (caseclaw/CHA-6) to work alongside an existing Polyphony/Stokowski autonomous agent workflow.
summary.outcome: gstack was installed globally and locally, CLAUDE.md was updated with gstack documentation, changes were committed to a feature branch, and a PR was created.

repo: The project (caseclaw) is essentially empty — just a .git directory and CLAUDE.md at the time of the session.
Stokowski is the orchestration framework managing the agent lifecycle (investigate → implement → review states) via Linear issues.
The project previously used Polyphony, which was removed in a prior commit before this session began.

code.finding: Updated with a gstack section documenting its installation and usage alongside the Stokowski workflow.

workflow: Stokowski uses Linear issues as a workpad — agents post investigation summaries and workpad updates as Linear comments.
The agent lifecycle has distinct stages: investigate → implement → review, each triggered by a separate prompt injection.
Linear API key access is required for posting comments; absence of env vars was a blocker encountered in the investigation stage.
gstack is installed both globally (npm/bun global) and copied into the project's .claude/skills/ directory.
The agent checks for existing branches and PRs before creating new ones to avoid duplication on rework runs.

friction: Linear API key / env vars were not available in the environment, blocking the investigation stage from posting its summary comment.
The investigation stage ended incomplete (no comment posted) and required a manual 'continue' prompt from the user to resume.
Multiple ToolSearch calls were needed because MCP plugin tools (mcp__plugin_context-mode_context-mode__ctx_*) had to be discovered at runtime — not known upfront.
The project had no source files or tests, so standard quality checks (type-check, lint, tests) had nothing to run against.
open_items: The investigation Linear comment may never have been successfully posted due to missing Linear API credentials — worth verifying the comment exists on CHA-6.
No tests exist for the gstack integration; the quality bar checklist items for type checking, linting, and tests were effectively skipped.
The relationship between gstack and the removed Polyphony system was not fully documented — future sessions should clarify how gstack replaces or extends Polyphony's functionality.
